steps.title: Decode what graph shapes mean
steps.content: A straight line with positive slope means constant speed away from home. A horizontal line means distance is unchanged (stationary). A straight line with negative slope means constant speed returning home. A curved line would indicate changing speed.

steps.title: Read the three segments
steps.content:
From $x = 0$ to $x = 1$: straight line rises from 0 to 60 miles — constant speed away from home.
From $x = 1$ to $x = 5$: horizontal line stays at 60 miles — stationary at the location for 4 hours.
From $x = 5$ to $x = 6$: straight line falls from 60 to 0 miles — constant speed returning toward home.

steps.title: Match to choices
steps.content:
Choices C and D describe curved segments (increasing/decreasing speed), but the graph shows only straight lines and one horizontal segment — eliminate C and D.

Choices A and B agree on the first two segments. Choice A claims segment 3 is "continued to drive away", but the graph shows distance falling from 60 to 0 miles, which means returning home, not moving away. Choice B claims "drove back to her home at a constant speed", matching the straight descending line perfectly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
